sections.executive_summary:
This proposal outlines a Fractional AI Officer engagement for [Company Name] — providing strategic AI leadership embedded in your business without the cost of a full-time hire. I'll serve as your Chief AI Officer on a part-time basis: building your AI roadmap, evaluating and implementing tools, managing AI vendor relationships, upskilling your team, and ensuring AI initiatives deliver measurable business outcomes.

Monthly investment: $5,500/month. 3-month minimum, recommended 6-month initial term.
sections.problem_statement:
[Company Name] is at an inflection point with AI. Leadership knows it's important. There are probably 5 different team members using AI tools independently. Someone's had a demo from 3 different vendors in the last 90 days. There might be an internal 'AI task force' that meets occasionally and doesn't produce decisions.

Without a clear owner, AI at [Company Name] will remain a collection of disconnected experiments. Some will work, most won't, and none will be connected to a coherent strategy. Six months from now you'll have spent $50,000 on AI tools, $20,000 in consulting fees, and achieved marginal impact — not because AI doesn't work, but because there was no one driving it with the right combination of technical knowledge and business judgment.

A full-time Chief AI Officer would solve this — but at $200,000–$300,000/year in base salary, plus equity, it's not the right hire for a company at your stage. The Fractional AI Officer model gives you the expertise without the overhead.
sections.proposed_solution:
**What I'll Do Each Month:**

**AI Roadmap Management:**
I'll maintain a living AI roadmap that connects every AI initiative to a business outcome. Each item has an owner, a timeline, a success metric, and a status. Leadership has full visibility at all times. I update it monthly and present a summary at our monthly strategy session.

**Tool Evaluation and Vendor Management:**
Every AI vendor that comes to [Company Name] goes through me first. I evaluate claims, check references, run POCs where warranted, and recommend. You stop buying tools that don't get used and start only committing to tools with clear use cases and owners.

**Team Enablement:**
I'll identify AI capability gaps in your team and address them — sometimes with training I deliver, sometimes by recommending external resources, sometimes by designing new workflows. The goal is that your team is becoming more capable of using AI over time, not more dependent on me.

**AI Project Oversight:**
For any AI project in flight — whether I'm implementing it or an external vendor is — I provide QA, progress review, and course correction. Projects don't drift; they deliver.

**Monthly Governance:**
AI raises legitimate questions about data privacy, security, and ethics in business use. I'll develop and maintain your AI usage policy, ensure compliance with relevant regulations, and advise on risk management for AI-generated outputs.

**Monthly Deliverables:**
- AI roadmap update (written)
- Monthly strategy session (90 minutes with leadership)
- Weekly async update (Loom video, 10 minutes, covering what I worked on and what's next)
- Quarterly board-ready AI summary (for investor or board use if relevant)
sections.deliverables_and_timeline:
**Month 1: Foundations**
- AI audit: inventory of all current AI tools and initiatives at [Company Name]
- Gap analysis: where is AI missing, where is it being used poorly
- Roadmap v1: prioritized list of AI opportunities with business case for each
- Policy: draft AI usage policy for review

**Month 2: Initiative Launch**
- 1–2 high-priority initiatives kicked off
- Vendor evaluations completed for any in-flight RFPs
- Team assessment completed; training plan developed

**Month 3+: Execution and Optimization**
- Regular cadence of roadmap updates, initiative oversight, team enablement
- Quarterly strategic review with leadership
sections.investment:
**Monthly Retainer: $5,500/month**
Includes approximately 10–12 hours of active engagement per month.

**What's included:**
- Monthly strategy session (90 min)
- Weekly async updates
- AI roadmap management
- Unlimited async questions (Slack/email, response within 24 hours on business days)
- Vendor evaluation (up to 2 per month)
- Team training sessions (up to 1 per month, included)
- Quarterly board AI summary

**What's not included (quoted separately):**
- Hands-on implementation work (building automations, configuring tools) — billed at $200/hour or quoted as separate projects
- In-person travel

**Commitment:** 3-month minimum. Most clients renew; the average engagement is 12–18 months.

**For context:** A full-time Chief AI Officer costs $200,000–$350,000/year in total compensation. An AI strategy consultant charges $300–$500/hour for tactical advice without ongoing accountability. This retainer gives you embedded strategic leadership at a fraction of either cost, with accountability tied to your actual business outcomes.
sections.team: [Your Name] — AI strategist and operator with [X years] experience implementing AI across [industries]. Previous roles or clients: [2–3 notable mentions]. Not a vendor of any specific AI tools — advice is always in your best interest.
sections.next_steps:
1. Confirm 3-month retainer term and start date
2. Sign service agreement (includes confidentiality provisions)
3. Pay month 1 retainer ($5,500)
4. AI audit kickoff call (2 hours) — I'll send a preparation guide the week before
5. Slack/Teams channel setup for ongoing async communication

**The audit call is the most important meeting we'll have.** Come prepared to talk candidly about what's been tried, what's failed, and where leadership is actually feeling pain. The more honest that conversation, the faster we can build something that works.

templates.variant: Professional Services Firm — AI Governance Focus

sections.executive_summary:
This proposal covers a Fractional AI Officer engagement for [Firm Name] with a specific focus on AI governance, risk management, and compliant AI adoption in a regulated environment. The goal: adopt AI aggressively where it helps, cautiously where regulation requires, and always with a clear policy framework that protects the firm.

Monthly investment: $6,500/month. Initial 6-month engagement recommended.
sections.problem_statement:
Professional services firms — law, accounting, financial advisory, healthcare-adjacent — face a unique AI dilemma. The efficiency opportunity is massive: AI can dramatically reduce time spent on research, document review, report generation, and client communication. But the risk is real: hallucination in client-facing work, data privacy concerns with client information in third-party AI systems, and regulatory uncertainty around AI-assisted professional advice.

Most firms in your position are doing one of two things: ignoring AI entirely (and falling behind) or adopting it haphazardly without a governance framework (and taking on risk). Neither is right.
sections.proposed_solution:
My role has two tracks running simultaneously:

**Track 1: Safe, High-Value Adoption**
Identify the AI applications inside [Firm Name] that are high-value, low-risk, and implementable now. Internal research, draft generation for attorney/advisor review, meeting summarization, internal knowledge retrieval, marketing and business development. These can be deployed quickly with appropriate guardrails and deliver significant efficiency gains.

**Track 2: Governance Framework**
Develop a comprehensive AI governance framework: which data can and cannot be used with AI tools, approved tool list with security assessment, staff training on appropriate use, quality control protocols for AI-assisted work product, and documentation practices that protect the firm.
sections.deliverables_and_timeline:
Month 1: AI audit + risk assessment + governance framework draft
Month 2: Low-risk high-value tools deployed + staff training on approved use
Month 3–6: Expanded adoption per governance framework + quarterly policy review
sections.investment: $6,500/month for an engagement that includes higher governance complexity and regulated environment advisory.
sections.team: [Your Name] with background in professional services AI implementation and familiarity with [relevant regulatory frameworks].
sections.next_steps: Start with a confidential 60-minute scoping call to understand your current AI exposure and risk posture. No commitment required for the scoping call.
